notes: Mistral Vibe maintains a single-session task list via the builtin todo tool (action:'write' = full-list replace; TodoStatus pending|in_progress|completed|cancelled; auto-executes with no permission gate). The driver asks vibe to create three todos and walk each pending→in_progress→completed. The lifecycle arc (ready→working→ready) and SubagentCount=0 are asserted as phases. The tasks-content observable — three items in the session API tasks field with matching subjects + status — now DOES surface: commit 0fccc242 taught core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/parser.go to decode the todo tool's todos[] through tailer.TodoReconciler into TaskDeltas/TaskSnapshot (the reference pattern is opencode's todowrite), so daemon=full. That content is verified structurally in the replay golden (summary.tasks), not the phase DSL, exactly as claudecode/codex/opencode. Observations assert tokens + model + agent only — Vibe carries no per-turn usage/cost, so cost is not asserted.

text: Vibe session (session_<ts>_<hex> dir) appears in ready; single-birth adapter so the first phase is anchored UNPINNED to start
PID bound shortly after launch via the CommandPattern matcher + DiscoverPID
User prompt drives the session ready → working

invariants: no state_transition to waiting for session
text: Session stays working across the todo create/write calls — todo is ToolPermission.ALWAYS (auto-executed, no permission gate fires); no child sessions spawn (SubagentCount stays 0)
Terminal no-tool assistant message drives turn_done: working → ready; final session state is ready; no child sessions were spawned
